target: decider self-apply path + apply-patches.js + workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH semantics + test.js schema coupling
title: Source-file rescue gap — decider's coherent multi-file commits silently drop their source-code half on universal-pusher rescue
problem: The universal-pusher rescue path (added 2026-04-26 commit 2379de4) rescues data/*.json + monitor/* files when decider's push 403s, but cannot rescue files in NEVER_PUSH (test.js, build.js, build-scripts/*, CLAUDE.md, monitor/prompts/*.md). When decider authors a coherent commit that crosses both buckets — e.g. data/predictions.json adds a new enum value AND test.js extends the validator array to accept it — and the push 403s, the data half survives and the source half is silently lost. origin/main now has the data delta but not the source delta, so tests fail at the next test cycle. Decider's daily report claims completion (in good faith — its local commit succeeded), but origin/main has only half the work. Operator must manually re-author the source half and force-resolve.

forensic_trace.method: Walked all decider/operator-rescue/PROP commits in main from 2026-04-18 through 2026-05-02 (14d window). For each, listed files touched. Filtered for commits that modified BOTH a data/*.json file AND a NEVER_PUSH source file (test.js, build.js, build-scripts/*, CLAUDE.md, monitor/prompts/*.md). Excluded docs/index.html and docs/assets/* because those are deterministic build products, not source-code changes that decider can author independently.

instances.commit_local: decider 13:19Z run 2026-05-02 (commit hash never reached origin/main; lost when clone destroyed)
instances.push_outcome: 403 — confirmed via monitor/integrity/push-failure-2026-05-02T13-22.json. Universal-pusher rescued 15 files to FUSE but workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH filter dropped test.js. Daily-report claims 'test.js updated to recognize tacit_withdrawal and dome_silent_terminal as valid enum values' but rescue commit ed521866 has no test.js entry.
instances.test_outcome: 2 test failures in main: 'PRED-105 author_status dome_silent_terminal is valid' FAIL, 'PRED-105 our_verdict tacit_withdrawal is valid' FAIL. Tests went 3518/0 → 3516/2 on main, undetected for ~3 hours until operator ran tests manually.
instances.operator_fix_summary: b2bfd743 added both new enum values to validAuthorStatuses + validOurVerdicts in test.js. Tests back to 3518/0. Manual fix only — structural bug remains.

instances.instance: INSTANCE-2 (clean push — no rescue gap, but same coupling shape)
instances.push_outcome: Successful push (commit lives in main). test.js delta added 'dome_silent' and 'silent_ignore' to the same validator arrays — exact same shape of change as INSTANCE-1, executed cleanly because the push didn't 403. This commit predates universal-pusher (2026-04-26) so the bug couldn't have manifested even on 403 — the prior workflow required operator manual rescue, which carries source via clone.
instances.implication: The pattern (decider extends a data/predictions.json enum AND extends the test.js validator array in the same commit) is RECURRING. We've seen it ~once per 12 days. INSTANCE-1 is the first time it intersected with universal-pusher's 403 path.

instances.push_outcome: Successful push. PROP-010 was the canonical case where decider self-applied a multi-file PROP that included a source-code change to build.js plus a prompt edit to decider-patches-and-selfapply.md plus data updates. Worked because no 403.
instances.implication: PROP integrations are the MOST EXPENSIVE class to lose if 403'd — they're large, multi-file, and human-authored intent. This is the second-rarest but highest-stakes shape of the bug.

instances.instance: INSTANCE-4 (operator rescue, source survived because of clone-based rescue)
instances.push_outcome: Decider 17:28Z push 403'd. Operator ran a manual full-clone rescue that included build-scripts/generate-html.js. Source survived because operator did the rescue from a clone, not via universal-pusher.
instances.implication: Pre-universal-pusher (≤2026-04-26), 403s required operator clone-rescue — which carried source. Post-universal-pusher, automated rescue covers data only. INSTANCE-1 is the first observed silent-drift case under the new regime. The bug is NEW (introduced 2026-04-26 commit 2379de4).

forensic_trace.incidence_estimate: 1 confirmed silent drift / 7 days post-universal-pusher (1 in window 2026-04-26 → 2026-05-02). Not zero, not high. Underlying coupling pattern (data + source in same commit) is ~1 per 12 days. Conditional on a 403, the silent-drift probability is high (most multi-file decider commits include source if they include schema or build-pipeline changes). 403 base rate over the 14d window: ~5 push failures (per push-failure-* logs) so roughly 36% of decider runs hit 403 at some point.
forensic_trace.underdetection_caveat: The forensic trace is structural (file paths in commit), not semantic (whether the lost source delta actually broke anything). INSTANCE-1 broke 2 tests immediately because test.js is the tightest coupling. A lost build-scripts/generate-html.js delta would cause UI mismatch (no test failure). A lost CLAUDE.md or monitor/prompts/*.md delta would cause silent doc/agent-behavior drift undetected by tests. So the OBSERVED test-failure rate is the LOWER BOUND on actual drift — silent doc/UI drift could be happening every other 403 and we wouldn't notice until much later.

taxonomy.summary: NEVER_PUSH files differ in coupling tightness to data/. The bug bites at different latency depending on which file type was lost.

buckets.drift_latency: immediate — tests fail on next 'node test.js' run
buckets.detection: decider self-apply pre-push test gate (catches in clone), workspace-sync pre-push test gate (catches at rescue time IF it runs tests), integrity agent daily 09:08Z run (catches within 24h), operator manual run (sporadic)
buckets.blast_radius: blocks decider's next push (pre-push gate fails); blocks workspace-sync's next sync (pre-push gate fails); CI-equivalent gate exists
buckets.fix_priority: HIGH — already bit us once in 7d

buckets.type: build-scripts/generate-html.js, build-scripts/generate-pdf.js, build-scripts/digest-reviews.js, build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js, build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js, build.js
buckets.drift_latency: next build cycle — 'node build.js html' produces wrong UI (e.g., new data field exists but template doesn't render it; or data has new shape but generator parses old shape and crashes)
buckets.detection: no automated test catches this. Visual inspection by operator OR integrity agent's build-drift check (which compares wins.json md5 to the embedded comment in docs/index.html — partial coverage). Build crashes are loud; silent UI mismatches are not.
buckets.blast_radius: live site shows stale or wrong content until operator notices and re-applies the source delta. No data corruption, no test failure cascade.
buckets.in_observed_window: 1 close-call (INSTANCE-4) — but rescued via operator clone-push, source survived
buckets.fix_priority: MEDIUM — worse than test.js because it's silent, but lower frequency and rescue often involves operator anyway

buckets.drift_latency: until next agent run on its schedule (minutes to 8h depending on agent), and even then only manifests if the prompt change was load-bearing
buckets.detection: no automated detection. Agents do not check 'does my prompt match what was intended.' Drift surfaces as agent behaving wrongly — caught by curmudgeon, integrity, or operator review of agent outputs.
buckets.blast_radius: wrong agent behavior on next run. Could be a missed dispatcher case, a stale schedule reference, etc. PROP integrations are the dominant authorship pattern here.
buckets.fix_priority: MEDIUM-HIGH — silent and behavior-affecting, and PROP integrations are exactly the class of work most likely to be lost (they're human-authored intent, expensive to re-author)

buckets.drift_latency: indefinite — only matters when a new session reads CLAUDE.md and inherits a stale fact
buckets.detection: tinker's CLAUDE.md accuracy audit (every Mode 3 run, daily-ish). No test catches this.
buckets.blast_radius: downstream agent confusion based on outdated context. Slow burn.
buckets.in_observed_window: 0 confirmed (CLAUDE.md was modified 7 times, but never in a multi-file data+CLAUDE.md commit that 403'd)
buckets.fix_priority: LOW — silent doc rot is real but tinker already audits this

taxonomy.headline_finding: The bug class is REAL but the OBSERVED instance count is LOW (1 confirmed in 7d). The IMPORTANT thing is that the latent surface area is large — a single 403 at the wrong moment can drop a load-bearing source-code change. The directive's framing is correct: this is structural, not anecdotal.

proposed_fix.summary: Three independent mechanisms ranked by recommendation strength. The recommended primary is Mechanism A (HALT-AND-REQUIRE-HUMAN), with Mechanism E (POST-RESCUE FAIL-LOUD GATE) as defense-in-depth. Mechanism D (SCHEMA-OUT-OF-SOURCE) is appealing but optional. Mechanism C (UNIVERSAL-PUSHER EXPANSION) is rejected. Mechanism B (DETECT-AND-WARN PRE-FLIGHT) is the weakest acceptable fallback.

mechanism_A_halt_and_require_human.name: HALT-AND-REQUIRE-HUMAN at decider's apply step
mechanism_A_halt_and_require_human.description: Modify decider's self-apply procedure (monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, Step 6b/3a) so that BEFORE committing, decider runs a pre-commit detection: 'git status --porcelain' finds any modified file in NEVER_PUSH (test.js, build.js, build-scripts/*, CLAUDE.md, monitor/prompts/*.md). If yes, decider STOPS, does NOT commit, writes the work to monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-NNN.json with requires_human_to_apply:true and a list of the source-file diffs, and exits cleanly with a daily-report note. Operator sees the request next cycle and applies in a clone where the push is reliable.

pros: Eliminates the bug at the source — the offending coherent commit is never created.
Aligns with existing patterns: verdict-change patches already require human, structural HTML changes already require human. Source-code changes are a natural extension of that policy.
Easy to verify mechanically (grep for the detect block).
Zero risk of widening NEVER_PUSH semantics.
No refactor of existing code.
Defense remains intact even if universal-pusher behavior changes — the halt is at the apply step, before any push.
cons: Decider loses ability to ship coherent multi-file changes autonomously. Operator becomes the bottleneck for any data+source coupled change.
Frequency assessment: ~1 such commit per 12 days observed in the window. Manageable for a human, but it's friction.
If decider has multi-source-file work queued (e.g. PROP-014-style rollouts), it can't ship them — must hand off.
Operator might forget to apply the suggested-patches file, leaving the work indefinitely stranded.
The detection is per-file-pattern; if a future NEVER_PUSH file is added but the detect list isn't updated, the bug returns.
edge_cases: What if decider's apply-patches.js script itself ONLY mutates data/ files (the documented behavior)? Then the source-file mutation must have come from a separate Edit tool call by the agent, which is decider stepping outside its sanctioned tool. That's worth flagging on its own — the halt should fire AND the daily report should note that decider attempted source-file authorship outside apply-patches.js.

What if the operator approves PROP-014-style rollouts where the entire intent is source-code edits + data + prompt edits in one cohesive commit? The PROP rollout pattern has historically been: tinker authors PROP file → operator approves → operator (or decider) applies. That should continue to work because the apply step is operator-driven, not decider-driven.

mechanism_A_halt_and_require_human.estimated_implementation_cost: 1-2 hour edit to decider-patches-and-selfapply.md (add pre-commit detect block + suggested-patches schema extension for source-file diffs); 30min smoke test in a clone.

mechanism_B_detect_and_warn_preflight.name: DETECT-AND-WARN PRE-FLIGHT in decider's daily report
mechanism_B_detect_and_warn_preflight.description: Same detection as Mechanism A, but softer: decider STILL applies the patch and STILL pushes. After push, if the commit included NEVER_PUSH source files, decider writes a HARD WARNING in the daily report summary calling out the multi-file commit and instructing the operator to verify both halves landed. The warning includes the rescue-failure scenario explicitly so operator knows to check on a 403.
mechanism_B_detect_and_warn_preflight.verification_pattern_post_implementation: grep -qE 'MULTI_FILE_SOURCE_DATA_WARNING|source-file rescue gap warning' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN

pros: Less coercive — decider can still ship the work autonomously if push succeeds.
Trivial to implement (add a warning to daily report).
Provides audit trail.
cons: DOES NOT FIX THE BUG. It just hopes operator notices the warning before the silent drift causes downstream failures.

Operator already has limited capacity to read every daily report. The warning will be noise on every multi-file commit, including the ones that pushed successfully — and operator will start ignoring it.

The bug ONLY manifests on 403. If push succeeded, the warning is wasted attention. If push 403'd, the daily report itself may not have been written (decider might have aborted before report-write step).
Doesn't help when integrity agent runs at 09:08Z next day — the 9-hour TTL is exactly the gap operator complained about.

mechanism_C_universal_pusher_expansion.name: UNIVERSAL-PUSHER EXPANSION (allow specific source files into the rescue list)
mechanism_C_universal_pusher_expansion.description: Whitelist test.js (and possibly build-scripts/*) into the universal-pusher rescue list. Modify workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH array to remove these files, and add corresponding entries to the universal-pusher rescue's smart_copy. workspace-sync would then push test.js / build-scripts/ deltas from FUSE → git when FUSE is newer.
mechanism_C_universal_pusher_expansion.verification_pattern_post_implementation: grep -A 30 'NEVER_PUSH=(' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md | grep -qE "'test\.js'" && echo STILL_BROKEN || echo FIXED

pros: Most direct fix — automatic rescue restored, no operator intervention.
Preserves coherent multi-file atomicity.
For test.js specifically, decider's pre-push test gate already proved the change is correct, so the round-trip is safe.
cons: VIOLATES NEVER_PUSH SEMANTICS. The whole reason NEVER_PUSH exists is that source code is git-owned and must NOT round-trip via FUSE — otherwise we have two writers (decider's clone, workspace-sync from FUSE) and the FUSE staleness window opens for source files. This was the architectural choice in PROP-013 / commit 2379de4 / HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001.
File-by-file expansion is brittle — every new schema-coupled source file (analyst.js, future test files, new build-scripts) needs to be added by hand or the bug returns.

Operator's stated lean (cited in directive proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker) is 'probably wrong tradeoff'. The directive's non_goal #3 explicitly demands a NEVER_PUSH-violation justification before this can be considered.

Even if test.js is whitelisted, what about cases where decider edits test.js intentionally vs unintentionally? Without semantic understanding, workspace-sync would push any FUSE test.js delta — including ones that came from random session writes.

mechanism_C_universal_pusher_expansion.recommendation: REJECT for the test.js case. The architectural cost (loosening NEVER_PUSH) outweighs the convenience win, and Mechanism A solves the same problem without the loosening. May be reconsidered ONLY if Mechanism A proves too friction-heavy in practice (e.g., operator measures >1 stranded suggested-patches per week and judges the cost of manual application as exceeding the cost of NEVER_PUSH erosion).

mechanism_D_schema_out_of_source.name: SCHEMA-OUT-OF-SOURCE — move test.js's validator arrays to data/schema-values.json
mechanism_D_schema_out_of_source.description: Refactor test.js so the validAuthorStatuses, validOurVerdicts, validEntryTypes, validTestability, validDerivation arrays are loaded from a new data file data/schema-values.json. test.js becomes 'const schemas = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync("data/schema-values.json","utf8"))' then uses schemas.validAuthorStatuses etc. When decider needs to add a new enum value, it patches data/schema-values.json (a data file, naturally rescuable). test.js is unchanged and the rescue path covers the new enum automatically.
mechanism_D_schema_out_of_source.verification_pattern_post_implementation: [ -f data/schema-values.json ] && grep -qE 'validAuthorStatuses\s*=\s*\[' test.js && echo STILL_BROKEN || ([ -f data/schema-values.json ] && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN)

pros: Eliminates the test.js-specific coupling permanently. Future enum additions never touch test.js.
Aligns with the pipeline's general direction (data is data, code reads data).
Data file can be reviewed by curmudgeon / integrity in the same way other data is reviewed.
Could be applied to other declarative schemas if they exist (validEntryTypes, etc., are already in the test.js arrays — same refactor moves them all).
cons: Doesn't help OTHER source-code coupling (build-scripts/*, build.js, monitor/prompts/*.md). Mechanism A is still needed for those.
Refactor cost — small but non-zero. Need to update test.js to read from the data file, write the data file, validate that all current values are preserved, run all tests.
Adds a new data file to maintain. Adds a new failure mode (data/schema-values.json malformed → tests can't even start).

If decider's new enum value goes into data/predictions.json AND data/schema-values.json in the same commit, that's still a multi-file commit but now both are data files — the rescue path covers both. Good. But if decider only patches predictions.json and forgets to patch schema-values.json, tests fail. Net: same failure mode, different file pair, but easier to detect (lint rule: every enum referenced in predictions.json must be in schema-values.json).
Operator described this as 'over-investment for one schema' in the directive note — fair pushback.

mechanism_D_schema_out_of_source.recommendation: OPTIONAL companion to Mechanism A. Worth doing IF operator agrees the test.js arrays are stable enough to commit to a one-time refactor. Independent of A — can ship in any order, no interaction. If shipped, INSTANCE-1's exact bug never recurs. If not shipped, INSTANCE-1's exact bug is prevented by Mechanism A's halt instead.
mechanism_D_schema_out_of_source.estimated_implementation_cost: 2-3 hours — small data file extraction + test.js refactor + verification that all tests still pass + integrity agent should reference the new data file.

mechanism_E_post_rescue_fail_loud_gate.name: POST-RESCUE FAIL-LOUD TEST GATE in workspace-sync
mechanism_E_post_rescue_fail_loud_gate.description: Modify workspace-sync (monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md, Step 3 or new Step 3.7) so that AFTER the universal-pusher rescue has staged its files but BEFORE the push, workspace-sync runs 'node test.js'. If tests fail, workspace-sync writes a CRITICAL alert to monitor/integrity/source-rescue-gap-alert-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM.json AND blocks the push (so the bad state doesn't propagate to main) AND notifies operator via human-facing summary. The alert names the failing tests, the data files in the rescue commit, and the suspected source-file delta that's missing.
mechanism_E_post_rescue_fail_loud_gate.verification_pattern_post_implementation: grep -qE 'POST_RESCUE_TEST_GATE|source-rescue-gap-alert' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN

pros: Catches the bug AT THE FIRST OCCURRENCE rather than 9h later via integrity agent.
Defense in depth — even if Mechanism A's halt is forgotten or Mechanism D's refactor doesn't ship, this catches real drift.
No NEVER_PUSH semantics violation.
Forces immediate operator attention rather than silent rot.
cons: Doesn't FIX the drift — just makes it loud. The data half is correct (decider's intent for predictions.json was right), but the push is now blocked because the source half is missing. Operator still has to manually apply the source delta. Result: longer outage of the rescue path until operator intervenes (vs Mechanism A's pattern where decider never tried to make the bad commit in the first place).

Could create a feedback loop: workspace-sync fails on missing test.js delta, alerts every hour, until operator manually pushes the source delta. This is loud but might be NOISIER than the current silent failure.

workspace-sync's environment: does it have node_modules to run tests? It's currently a Haiku-tier agent doing minimal work. Adding a full test run might make workspace-sync slower / more expensive / more likely to fail for unrelated reasons.

Tests passing locally in workspace-sync's clone doesn't actually mean main is good — workspace-sync's clone has the rescue commit applied but main doesn't yet. Need to be careful about WHICH state we're testing against.
Risk: workspace-sync's pre-push gate already exists (PROP-013 era) for STRUCTURAL invariants. Adding behavioral test runs is a meaningful escalation in scope.

mechanism_E_post_rescue_fail_loud_gate.recommendation: Defense-in-depth backstop AFTER Mechanism A. Implement only if Mechanism A proves insufficient (e.g., decider's halt detection has a gap and a real silent-drift slips through despite A). Independent of A and D.

recommended_primary.rationale: It's the simplest defensive fix. It catches the bug at the source (the act of authoring the offending coherent commit) rather than at the rescue path. It aligns with existing patterns where decider already hands off non-mechanical changes to humans. It doesn't touch NEVER_PUSH semantics. It doesn't require a refactor. The estimated friction (1 stranded suggested-patches per ~12 days) is well below operator's pain threshold — operator already manually applies PROP integrations at higher cadence than that. The verification pattern is a clean grep against the prompt file. If A doesn't fully suffice — e.g., we observe a real source-file drift slipping through within 30 days — Mechanism E adds defense-in-depth at relatively low cost. Mechanism D ships independently and gives extra coverage for the test.js-schema case specifically; recommended IF the operator agrees the test.js validator arrays are stable enough to commit to a one-time data extraction. Mechanism C is rejected per the directive's non-goal #3 unless a compelling NEVER_PUSH-violation justification is constructed.
recommended_primary.secondary_recommendation: Mechanism E (defense-in-depth) — implement after A has been in production 30 days and operator can decide whether the residual risk warrants the extra workspace-sync complexity.
recommended_primary.tertiary_recommendation: Mechanism D (schema extraction) — useful, optional; ship if operator agrees the test.js arrays are settled.

measurement_plan.primary_metric: Source-file drift incidents in origin/main per 14-day window. Defined as: any commit in main where (a) a data file added a value not represented in the corresponding source-code validator/handler, AND (b) it was caused by decider authorship (not operator manual edit). Detected by running 'node test.js' daily on main HEAD and counting any failure attributable to enum/value mismatches.
measurement_plan.baseline_pre_implementation: 1 incident in 7 days (INSTANCE-1, 2026-05-02 PRED-105 schema drift). Extrapolating linearly: ~26 per year. NOT a high rate but unacceptable given the silent-rot variants in build-scripts and prompts that we DON'T detect with tests.
measurement_plan.target_post_implementation_A: Zero source-file drift incidents in main over 30d post-rollout. Disprove condition: ≥1 drift incident in 30d → Mechanism A's halt detection has a gap (likely: decider edited a NEVER_PUSH file via a path the detect block didn't anticipate). Diagnose gap, patch detect logic.
measurement_plan.target_post_implementation_E: Zero NEW source-file drift incidents that the integrity agent or tinker had to discover later. If E catches 0 drifts in 30d, Mechanism A is doing its job. If E catches ≥1 drift in 30d, A has a gap and E is rescuing it.
measurement_plan.secondary_metric: Decider's stranded suggested-patches rate. How many suggested-patches files are written with requires_human_to_apply:true per 7d that operator has NOT applied within 48h of authorship. Target: <1 per 7d. If higher, Mechanism A is creating real friction for the operator and we should reconsider scope (e.g., add Mechanism D specifically to remove the most-frequent class).
measurement_plan.tertiary_metric: False-positive rate on Mechanism A's halt. Cases where the halt fired but the change was actually safe (e.g., a comment-only edit to a build-script). Target: <10% of halts. If higher, refine the detect block (e.g., check 'git diff --stat' and skip if all source-file changes are comments-only).

sequencing.order: D (independent, optional) -- A (recommended primary) -- E (defense-in-depth, optional)

interactions.A_alone: Sufficient to prevent INSTANCE-1's class of bug. Ships first.
interactions.A_plus_D: A prevents the offending commit from being created; D additionally removes the test.js-specific coupling so it CAN'T happen for the most acute case even if A's detect block has a gap. Belt and suspenders for the highest-blast-radius file.
interactions.A_plus_E: A is the primary defense; E catches anything that slips through. E should NOT ship before A — without A, E creates noise alerts without solving the underlying authorship problem.
interactions.A_plus_D_plus_E: All three together. Most defensive. Recommended only if operator measures real residual risk after A alone is in production.
interactions.C_excluded: Mechanism C rejected; not in the sequence.

independence.D_independent_of_A: Yes. D is a refactor of test.js; doesn't touch decider's authorship behavior. Can ship before A, after A, or alongside A.
independence.E_dependent_on_A: Recommended after A. E without A produces alert noise without root-cause fix.
independence.A_dependent_on_nothing: A is self-contained — single edit to decider-patches-and-selfapply.md.

sequencing.first_step: Operator approves PROP-016. Tinker (or operator) implements Mechanism A: edit monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md to add the NEVER_PUSH pre-commit detect block + suggested-patches schema extension. 30-day monitoring period. Then operator decides on D and E.

tradeoffs_meta.what_we_gain: Eliminate silent source-file drift in main. Move decider from 'try to ship coherent multi-file changes and hope the push succeeds' to 'recognize coherent multi-file changes and route source-code halves to humans.' Preserve NEVER_PUSH semantics as a hard architectural invariant rather than letting it erode case-by-case.
tradeoffs_meta.what_we_might_lose: Some autonomy. Decider can no longer ship (data + source) coherent changes without operator. Frequency: ~1 per 12d per the trace. Operator becomes the rate-limiter on PROP-style multi-file integrations.
what_could_go_wrong: Mechanism A's detect block has a gap — a future NEVER_PUSH file is added but the detect list isn't updated → bug returns. MITIGATION: have the detect block read from workspace-sync.md's NEVER_PUSH array dynamically rather than hardcoding paths.

Operator stops applying stranded suggested-patches files because there are too many or because operator forgets. MITIGATION: tinker's Mode 1 pipeline-health check counts unapplied suggested-patches >48h old; alerts if >2.

Mechanism A is too coarse — fires on cosmetic source-file edits (comment fixes, whitespace) that don't actually need human review. MITIGATION: tertiary metric tracks false-positive rate; refine if >10%.

Operator approves Mechanism A but never gets to D or E; the test.js coupling remains and a future enum-add bug recurs. MITIGATION: D is the long-term cleanup but A covers the failure; if A holds, D becomes nice-to-have, not required.
what_we_assume: 403 push failures will continue at observed rate (~5 per 14d). If GitHub fixes whatever's causing 403s for non-steve-author commits, the bug becomes purely theoretical. We're proposing a fix for an environmental bug in our pipeline; if the environment heals, the fix becomes redundant but not harmful.

Decider's coherent multi-file authorship pattern will continue. If the project moves to a different model (e.g., decider becomes purely data-only and all source edits go through a separate authorial flow), Mechanism A becomes overkill. Current trajectory says decider keeps doing PROP-014 / PROP-016-style work, so the assumption holds for now.
Operator can absorb 1 stranded suggested-patches per 12d. If operator capacity drops, this assumption fails.

estimated_impact.drift_incidents_prevented_per_year: ~26 (extrapolating INSTANCE-1's 1-per-7d cadence to a year). Conservative — true rate is higher because silent build-scripts/prompts drift would be caught by A but isn't currently detected.
estimated_impact.operator_friction_added: ~30 manual applications per year (1 per 12d cadence of multi-file-source+data commits). Each application = ~10min in a clone. Net: ~5 hours/year operator time added.

why_human_needed: Operator must decide: (1) approve Mechanism A as primary; (2) decide whether to also ship D (test.js refactor) immediately or wait 30d to see if A is sufficient; (3) decide whether to ship E proactively or only as escalation. Tinker's recommendation is A immediately, D optional alongside, E only if A proves insufficient.

non_goals_honored.no_decider_push_reliability_changes: PROP-016 does not propose changing decider's PAT, retry behavior, or any push-environment variable. Per directive non_goal #1.
non_goals_honored.no_prop_014_changes: PROP-016 does not modify PROP-014 mechanisms or amend it. Per directive non_goal #2. PROP-014 is a sibling bug for state-coupling drift; PROP-016 addresses the disjoint case of source-file rescue gap.
non_goals_honored.universal_pusher_widening_argued_then_re: Mechanism C explicitly considers and rejects widening NEVER_PUSH. Per directive non_goal #3.
non_goals_honored.scope_held_to_NEVER_PUSH_class: PROP-016 does not address data-only inter-file coherence (already handled by existing decider patterns). Per directive non_goal #4.

implementation_notes: Mechanism A's exact text for decider-patches-and-selfapply.md will be drafted in the implementation step (separate authorization). Sketch: insert a new pre-commit subsection after Step 6b's 'node build-scripts/apply-patches.js' call and before 'git add data/ docs/ downloads/ monitor/'. Block uses 'git status --porcelain' to find any modified file matching the dynamic NEVER_PUSH list (read from workspace-sync.md array). If any matches, write monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-NNN.json with requires_human_to_apply:true, log to daily report, exit 0 (do NOT commit, do NOT push). If no source-file matches, proceed with normal commit + push flow as today.
